channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Samsung's Galaxy Watch sleep apnea detection could get a big future boost from AI
item.description: Samsung is partnering with Stanford University to try and improve its sleep apnea detection feature using AI.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Dan Clark Audio's Noire X are the Johnny Cash of elite planar magnetic headphones and I must have them
item.description: Looking for the ultimate Man in Black cans? You just found them, although the price-tag may still (ahem) Hurt…
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: New US CIO appointments, April 2025
item.description: Movers & Shakers is where you can keep up with new CIO appointments and gain valuable insight into the job market and CIO hiring trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stop typing in the Start menu
item.description: The Windows Start menu isn’t the best search tool when it comes to professional productivity. Sure, it’s convenient — it’s better than clicking your way through the entire menu to find what you need. But more often than not, it mostly just gets in your way.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: A wild new way to think about Android apps
item.description: Fair warning: This column is a fair bit different from the type of thinking we typically take on in these musty virtual quarters of ours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Copilot in Azure reaches general availability
item.description: 11 months after hitting public preview, Copilot in Azure has just been signed off as a generally available product.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Marvel Rivals next season release date and Season 1.5 end date
item.description: Here's when to expect Marvel Rivals Season 2, as well as some info on the new Heroes: Emma Frost and Ultron. Also, a look at upcoming changes to Team Ups, Competitive Mode, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5060 series shipping leak reveals the same memory bus as last-gen - but it's not as bad as you may think
item.description: A new shipping description suggests Nvidia's RTX 5060 GPU series may use the same memory bus as the RTX 4060 series, but with GDDR7 VRAM instead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Man who used AI lawyer explains why he did so after judge got angry
item.description: Dewald maintains he received prior approval for his presentation method, though not specifically for an AI-generated speaker.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Federal Prison Sentence for Silicon Valley AI Startup Founder
item.description: Shaukat Shamim, founder of YouPlus, sentenced to 2½ years in federal prison for securities fraud and ordered to pay a $50,000 fine.
item.dc:creator: Jason Green, The Mercury News

item.title: What misleading Meta Llama 4 benchmark scores show enterprise leaders about evaluating AI performance claims
item.description: Benchmarks are critical when evaluating AI — they reveal how well models work, as well as their strengths and weaknesses, based on factors like reliability, accuracy, and versatility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From training dogs to intelligent machines: Here’s how reinforcement learning is teaching AI
item.description: Understanding intelligence and creating intelligent machines are grand scientific challenges of our times. The ability to learn from experience is a cornerstone of intelligence for machines and living beings alike.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Broadcom favors stock buyback over investing in innovation
item.description: Broadcom plans to spend its cash pile on buying back as much as $10 billion worth of its common stock, dampening IT department hopes that it might invest in innovation or make its products more affordable.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Beware these 10 malicious VS Code extensions
item.description: Developers using Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code (VSCode) editor are being warned to delete, or at least stay away from, 10 newly published extensions which will trigger the installation of a cryptominer.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: An Intel-TSMC deal could reshape x86 future and enterprise chip supply chains
item.description: Intel is reportedly in advanced discussions with TSMC to form a joint venture that could potentially reshape the future of the x86 platform and the global semiconductor landscape.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration
item.description: Max Everett, a former Energy Department CIO, offers some advice for how new agency technology leaders can deal with some basic challenges. The post Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Appeals panel clears way for DOGE access to sensitive personal data at OPM, Education Department
item.description: A federal appeals panel on Monday paused an order curtailing the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies, reopening the floodgates for the cost-cutting advisory board. In a 2-1 decision, a panel of judges on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: President Trump on Monday threatened to impose a 50 percent tariff on imports from China, a massive escalation of a potential trade war between the world's two largest economies. Trump's threat is the latest tit for tat between the U.S. and China in the last week.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Dangers of oversharing with AI tools
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says ChatGPT learns from chats, but you should avoid sharing sensitive information to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google NotebookLM cheat sheet: Get fast insights into your docs
item.description: When Google first launched the NotebookLM beta in late 2023, the company billed it as an experimental note-taking app with a generative AI assistant built in. You can still use it to create notes, but the app has evolved into something much more novel and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Zero-emissions hydrogen-electric jet disrupts conventional flights
item.description: Beyond Aero's BYA-1: Hydrogen-electric jet with zero emissions, reduced costs, 2030 launch. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses a potential game changer in the evolution of air travel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI will require more software developers, not fewer
item.description: It’s time we officially bury the idea that AI means we won’t need software developers. For years, we’ve heard that generative AI and large language models (LLMs) are coming for our jobs. In software development, that supposedly means fewer humans writing code. That’s not what the data suggests.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GOP rep says Russia and China are 'laughing at us' after NSA director firing
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.), a moderate Republican, slammed President Trump’s decision to fire the director of the National Security Agency (NSA) and his top deputy after demands from far-right influencer Laura Loomer, saying Sunday that China and Russia are “laughing at us” after that decision.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The best ways to give your old Android a second life
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how you can creatively repurpose your old unused Android devices, which are full of potential and hidden value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Battlestar Galactica' is 'even more relevant now,' star says
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump extends TikTok sale deadline again—this time by 75 days
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nintendo delays Switch 2 US preorders over Trump tariffs
item.description: Nintendo said U.S. preorders of its Switch 2 console will not take place on April 9 as it assesses tariff implications.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5
